title: Nova Sustainable Fuels Receives Approval to Produce Sustainable Aviation Fuel in Guysborough County
content: Nova Sustainable Fuels has received environmental assessment approval for the first phase of its renewable energy park in Goldboro, Guysborough County. The facility will produce sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using waste biomass from Nova Scotiaâ€™s forestry and forest-based industries, providing a low-emission alternative to petroleum fuels. Minister Timothy Halman praised the project for reducing global emissions and boosting the local economy. It is projected to create approximately 1,000 jobs during construction and up to 80 operational positions through 2081. The approval comes with 34 stringent terms and conditions to ensure environmental protection. However, the company still requires a second environmental assessment approval for Phase 2, which proposes a wind and solar farm to power the facility. This initiative addresses Nova Scotiaâ€™s biomass oversupply while advancing the provinceâ€™s clean energy goals.
author: Analysts Account
